title: The Notice that is called the Spring by Emily Dickinson — Nature Poetry Lines
content:
The Notice that is called the Spring
Is but a month from here --
Put up my Heart thy Hoary work
And take a Rosy Chair.

Not any House the Flowers keep --
The Birds enamor Care --
Our salary the longest Day
Is nothing but a Bier.
meaning: "The Notice that is called the Spring" by Emily Dickinson is a nature poem consisting of 9 lines. The poet explores themes of nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "The Notice that is called the Spring Is but a month from here --...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emily Dickinson's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
